q: Coral reefs have a very high (A. ecosystem B. plants C. populations D. biodiversity). (2026, 2025)
The number of species in a community is known as species (A. evenness B. richness C. frequency D. diversity). (2025)
Genetic diversity is the variety of genes within a (A. ecosystem B. species C. community D. population). (2026, 2025)
All organisms in an ecosystem can affect the (A. physical B. natural C. environmental D. biological) conditions around them. (2025)
Forest ecosystems are a (A. carbon B. oxygen C. hydrogen D. nitrogen) sink and stabilize soils. (2025)
The diverse habitats are home for a wide range of (A. kingdom B. domain C. genus D. species). (2025)
Forest ecosystems are a (A. oxygen B. nitrogen C. carbon D. hydrogen) sink and stabilize soils. (2026)
(A. Dry forest B. Deciduous forest C. Rain forest D. Swamp forest) occurs where rainfall is usually less than 400 mm a year. (2025)
Rhododendron arboretum is the (A. rare B. endangered C. endemic D. threaten) plant species in Myanmar. (2025)
The Red Data Book includes the list of endangered species of (A. plants, B. animals, C. plants and animals fossils, D. plants and animals). (2024)
The common nameof Linpai is (A. Asian Elephant, B. Eld's Deer, C. Irrawaddy Dolphin, D. Green Peafowl). (2024)
A variety of human activities threatens the (A. biodiversity B. ecosystem C. environment D. agrobiodiversity) of Myanmar. (2026)
Habitats become divided into small areas known as habitat (A. fragmentation B. loss C. exploitation D. degradation). (2025)
Habitat destruction has played a key role in (A. extinction B. extension C. existing D. extant). (2025)
The destruction of the natural environment leads to (A. habitat change B. habitat fragmentation C. habitat diversity D habitat loss). (2025)
Habitat loss is the most significant cause of (A. agrobiodiversity, B. biodiversity, C. water, D. forest) loss globally. (2024, 2025)
Climate change is a growing threat to (A. species diversity, B. biodiversity, C. ecosystem diversity, D. genetic diversity). (2024, 2025)
Wildlife trade can involve the trade of (A. living, B. fossil, C. living or dead, D. dead) individuals. (2024, 2025)
Pangolins are the only (A. egg laying B. flying C. scaly D. reptilian) mammal on earth. (2025)
More than one million pangolins were poached and killed for their (A. scales B. tail C. bones D. internal organs). (2025)
Wildlife smuggling directly affects the (A. wildlife B. biodiversity C. conservation D. invasive) of different ecosystems. (2025)
The most trafficked animal in the world is the (A. rhinoceros, B. pangolin, C. tiger, D. elephant). (2024)
Seeds are kept in cold, dark conditions in a (A. seed store B. seed building C. seed house D. seed bank). (2025)
Dried seeds are stored in a temperature of -14°C and in (A. light B. dark C. shade D. dim-light) conditions. (2025)
Botanical gardens play an important role as valuable sources of plant (A. physiology B. taxonomy C. anatomy D. ecology). (2025)
(A. Aquarium, B. Botanical, C. Aviary, D. Zoological) gardens are ideal places to integrate the study and conservation of trees species that are endangered in the wild. (2024)
Seed banks protect and save (A. botanical gardens, B. ecosystem diversity, C. wildlife trafficking, D. plant genetic diversity). (2024)
(A. Ecological park B. National park C. Private park D. Urban park ) is an area set aside by a national government for the preservation of the natural environment. (2025)
A marine park is a permanent marine (A. reservation, B. isolation, C. sanctuary, D. biosphere) for the conservation of species. (2024)
A wildlife sanctuary is an area where (A. plant, B. animal, C. forest, D. natural) habitats. (2024)
Hlawga park is a type of (A. National Park, B. Scientific reserve C. Wildlife sanctuary D. Community forest). (2026, 2024)
